# paper7

> CLI tool that fetches arXiv papers as clean Markdown for LLM context. Zero dependencies beyond curl/sed/grep/awk.

## Why

Raw PDFs waste tokens and hurt LLM comprehension. paper7 extracts clean, structured Markdown from arXiv papers — 97% smaller than PDF, 86% smaller than raw HTML. Research shows that noisy, long contexts degrade LLM performance by 20%+ (Liu et al., "Lost in the Middle", 2023).

## Install

```
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lucianfialho/paper7/main/install.sh | bash
```

## Commands

- `paper7 search <query>` — Search arXiv papers by keyword. Options: `--max N` (default 10), `--sort relevance|date`
- `paper7 get <id>` — Fetch paper, convert to clean Markdown, cache locally. Options: `--no-refs` (strip references), `--no-cache` (force re-download)
- `paper7 repo <id>` — Find GitHub repository URLs linked in the paper
- `paper7 list` — Show all cached papers with ID and title
- `paper7 cache clear [id]` — Clear all cache or remove specific paper

## Notes

- Papers are fetched from ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org (HTML version of arXiv, no PDF parsing)
- Output is clean Markdown with proper headers (##), paragraphs, bold/italic
- Cached at `~/.paper7/cache/<id>/paper.md`
- Accepts arXiv ID (2401.04088) or full URL (https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088)
- Output goes to stdout — pipe to any LLM, clipboard, or file
